requirements.full_sentence:
These directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning)
Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
requirements.object: immediate effect

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to All India Financial Institutions (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as an 'AIFI') viz.
requirements.object: India Financial Institutions (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'AIFIs' and individually as an 'AIFI') viz

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall also follow the prudential guidelines on income recognition, asset
classification and provisioning of advances for restructured accounts as
prescribed in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions -
Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025, in addition to these Directions.
requirements.object: guidelines on income recognition, asset classification and provisioning of advances for restructured accounts as prescribed

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meaning assigned to them below:
(1) 'crop season' for each crop, shall mean the period up to harvesting of
the crops raised, as determined by the State Level Bankers' Committee
(SLBC) in each State;
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'crop season' for each crop, shall mean the period up to harvesting of the crops raised, as determined by

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'doubtful asset' shall mean an asset which has remained in the
substandard category for a period of twelve months;
requirements.object: has remained in the substandard category for a period of twelve months

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'exposure' shall include all funded and non-funded exposures (including
underwriting and similar commitments).
requirements.object: non-funded exposures (including underwriting and similar commitments)

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) 'long duration crops' shall mean crops with crop season longer than one
year;
requirements.object: season longer than one year

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) 'loss asset' shall mean an asset where loss has been identified by an
AIFI or internal or external auditors or the inspection conducted by the
Reserve Bank of India, but the amount has not been written off wholly
by the AIFI;
requirements.object: loss has been identified by an AIFI or internal or external auditors or the inspection conducted by the Reserve Bank of India, but

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) 'non-performing asset' shall mean an asset, including a leased asset,
which has ceased to generate income for an AIFI;
requirements.object: a leased asset, which has ceased to generate income for an AIFI

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) 'overdue' status - any amount due to a AIFI under any credit facility shall
be treated as 'overdue' if it is not paid on the due date fixed by the AIFI.
requirements.object: if it is not paid on the due date fixed by the AIFI
provisioning to gross non-performing assets and indicates the extent of funds kept aside to cover loan losses

requirements.full_sentence:
(9) 'security' shall mean tangible security properly charged to the AIFI and
will not include intangible securities like guarantees (including State
government guarantees), comfort letters, etc.
requirements.object: charged to the AIFI and will not include intangible securities like guarantees (including State government guarantees)

requirements.full_sentence:
(10) 'short duration crops' shall mean crops which are not 'long duration'
crops;
requirements.object: not 'long duration' crops

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(11) 'substandard asset' shall mean an asset, which has remained NPA for a
period less than or equal to twelve months;
requirements.object: has remained NPA for a period less than or equal to twelve months

conditions:
where the realisable
value of the security
requirements.full_sentence:
(12) 'unsecured exposure' shall mean an exposure where the realisable
value of the security, as assessed by a AIFI / approved valuers / the
Reserve Bank's inspecting officers, is not more than ten percent, ab-
initio, of the outstanding exposure.
requirements.object: the realisable value of the security, as assessed by a AIFI / approved valuers / the Reserve Bank's inspecting officers, is not

requirements.full_sentence:
(13) 'wilful defaulter' shall mean the same as defined under the Reserve Bank
of India (All India Financial Companies - Treatment of Wilful Defaulters
and Large Defaulters) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: defined under the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Companies - Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters) Directi

requirements.full_sentence:
The terms 'micro enterprises', 'small enterprises', and 'medium enterprises' shall
have the same meaning as defined in circular FIDD.MSME &
NFS.BC.No.3/06.02.31/2020-21 dated July 2, 2020 on 'Credit flow to Micro,
Small and Medium Enterprises Sector' as updated from time to time.
requirements.object: as defined in circular FIDD

requirements.full_sentence:
The terms 'credit event' and 'default' shall have the same meaning as defined in
the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of
Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
The terms 'Commercial Real Estate (CRE)', 'Commercial Real Estate -
Residential Housing Sector (CRE - RH)', 'project finance', and 'financial closure'
shall have the same meaning as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (All India
Financial Institutions - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: as defined in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions unless defined herein shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or the Companies Act, 2013, or any statutory
modification or re-enactment thereto or other regulations issued by the Reserve
Bank or the Glossary of Terms published by the Reserve Bank or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or the Companies Ac

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall frame a Board approved policy for the implementation of the
regulations contained in this Chapter, which shall inter alia include the following
principles / components:
(1) the policy of income recognition shall be objective and based on record
of recovery rather than on any subjective considerations;
requirements.object: policy for the implementation of the regulations contained in this Chapter, which shall inter alia include the following princi

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of Directors of an AIFI shall take all necessary steps to arrest the
deteriorating asset quality in the books and focus on improving the credit risk
management system.
requirements.object: to arrest the deteriorating asset quality in the books and focus on improving the credit risk management system

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall ensure that realistic repayment schedules are fixed on the basis of
cash flows in consultation with borrowers while granting loans and advances, to
facilitate prompt repayment by the borrowers and thus improve the record of
recovery in advances.
requirements.object: schedules are fixed on the basis of cash flows in consultation with borrowers while granting loans and advances, to fa

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall comply with the following instructions in respect of all loans
sanctioned on or after December 31, 2021:
(1) The exact due dates for repayment of a loan, frequency of repayment,
breakup between principal and interest, examples of dates of
classification as special mention account (SMA) / non-performing asset
(NPA), etc.
requirements.object: instructions in respect of all loans sanctioned on or after December 31, 2021: (1) The exact due dates for repayment of a lo

requirements.full_sentence: shall be clearly specified in the loan agreement;
requirements.object: the loan agreement

conditions:
if any, to the sanction terms
/ loan agreement till full repayment of the loan
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) The borrower shall be apprised of the same at the time of loan sanction
and also at the time of subsequent changes, if any, to the sanction terms
/ loan agreement till full repayment of the loan;
requirements.object: same at the time of loan sanction and also at the time of subsequent changes

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In cases of loan facilities with moratorium on payment of principal and /
or interest, the exact date of commencement of repayment shall also be
specified in the loan agreements.
requirements.object: the loan agreements

conditions: In case of loans sanctioned before December 31
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of loans sanctioned before December 31, 2021, compliance to the
instructions in paragraph 13 shall be ensured as and when such loans become
due for renewal / review.
requirements.object: when such loans become due for renewal / review

requirements.full_sentence:
Stock audit at annual intervals by external agencies appointed as per the
guidelines approved by the Board shall be mandatory in cases of NPAs with
balance of ₹5 crore and above in order to enhance the reliability on stock
valuation and bring down divergence arising out of difference in assessment of
the value of security.
requirements.object: of NPAs with balance of ₹5 crore and above in order to enhance the reliability on stock valuation and bring down divergence aris

requirements.full_sentence:
In cases of NPAs with balance of ₹5 crore and above, collateral such as
immovable properties charged in favour of the AIFI shall be valued once in three
years by valuers appointed as per the guidelines approved by the Board of
Directors.
requirements.object: three years by valuers appointed as per the guidelines approved by the Board of Directors

conditions:
if no reply / satisfactory clarification is received
from them within one month
requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall seek explanation from advocates who wrongly certify as to clear
legal titles in respect of assets or valuers who overstate the security value, by
negligence or connivance, and if no reply / satisfactory clarification is received
from them within one month, they may report their names to IBA.
requirements.object: who wrongly certify as to clear legal titles in respect of assets or valuers who overstate the security value, by negl

requirements.full_sentence:
IBA may
circulate the names of such advocates / valuers among its members for
consideration before availing of their services in future.
requirements.object: such advocates / valuers among its members for consideration before availing of their services in future

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall implement the following instructions to increasing awareness
among the borrowers:
(1) place consumer education literature on its website, explaining with
examples, the concepts of date of overdue, special mention account and
non-performing asset classification and upgradation, with specific
reference to day-end process;
requirements.object: to increasing awareness among the borrowers: (1) place consumer education literature on its website, explaining w

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) consider displaying such consumer education literature in its branches
by means of posters and / or other appropriate media;
requirements.object: or other appropriate media

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall make suitable disclosures in its Notes to Accounts as per the
requirements contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions,
2025.
requirements.object: its Notes to Accounts as per the requirements contained in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions -

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall classify a loan or an advance as a standard asset or a NPA, as the
case may be.
requirements.object: an advance as a standard asset or a NPA, as the case may be

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall put in place a robust Management Information System (MIS)
mechanism for early detection of signs of distress at individual account level as
well as at segment level (asset class, industry, geographic, size, etc.).
requirements.object: robust Management Information System (MIS) mechanism for early detection of signs of distress at individual account level as well as at

requirements.full_sentence:
Such early
warning signals should be used for putting in place an effective preventive asset
quality management framework, including a transparent restructuring
mechanism for viable accounts under distress within the prevailing regulatory
framework, for preserving the economic value of those entities in all segments.
requirements.object: in place an effective preventive asset quality management framework, including a transparent restructuring mechanism for viable ac

requirements.full_sentence:
The Information Technology (IT) and MIS system of an AIFI should be robust
and able to generate reliable and quality information with regard to their asset
quality for effective decision making.
requirements.object: to generate reliable and quality information with regard to their asset quality for effective decision making

requirements.full_sentence:
There should be no inconsistencies
between information furnished under regulatory / statutory reporting and their
own MIS reporting.
requirements.object: information furnished under regulatory / statutory reporting and their own MIS reporting

conditions:
if so, as part of its day-end
processes for the due date
requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall flag a borrower account as overdue, if so, as part of its day-end
processes for the due date, irrespective of the time of running such processes.
requirements.object: as overdue

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, classification of borrower accounts as SMA as well as NPA shall be
done as part of day-end process for the relevant date and the SMA or NPA
classification date shall be the calendar date for which the day end process is
run.
requirements.object: of day-end process for the relevant date and the SMA or NPA classification date shall be the calendar date for which the day end proce

requirements.full_sentence:
Thus, the date of SMA / NPA shall reflect the asset classification status of
an account at the day-end of that calendar date.
requirements.object: status of an account at the day-end of that calendar date

conditions: If due date of a loan account is March 31
requirements.full_sentence:
Illustration I:
If due date of a loan account is March 31, 2021, and full dues are
not received before the AIFI runs the day-end process for this date, the date of
overdue shall be March 31, 2021.

conditions: If it continues to remain overdue
requirements.full_sentence:
If it continues to remain overdue, then this
account shall get tagged as SMA-1 upon running day-end process on April 30,
2021 i.e.
requirements.object: upon running day-end process on April 30, 2021 i

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordingly,
the date of SMA-1 classification for that account shall be April 30, 2021.

conditions: if the account continues to remain overdue

if continues to remain
overdue further
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Similarly, if the account continues to remain overdue, it shall get tagged as SMA-
2 upon running day-end process on May 30, 2021 and if continues to remain
overdue further, it shall get classified as NPA upon running day-end process on
June 29, 2021.
requirements.object: 2 upon running day-end process on May 30, 2021 and if continues to remain overdue further, it shall get classified as NPA upon runn

requirements.full_sentence:
The classification of assets of an AIFI as non-performing shall be done on the
basis of objective criteria to ensure a uniform and consistent application of the
norms, and by taking into account the degree of well-defined credit weaknesses.
requirements.object: basis of objective criteria to ensure a uniform and consistent application of the norms, and by taking into account the degree of well-

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI should have system generated segment wise information on NPAs and
restructured assets which may include data on the opening balances, additions,
reductions (upgradations, actual recoveries, write-offs etc.), closing balances,
provisions held, technical write-offs, etc.
requirements.object: opening balances, additions, reductions (upgradations, actual recoveries, write-offs etc

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall compute their Gross Advances, Net Advances, Gross NPAs and
Net NPAs, as per the format in Annex - I.
requirements.object: Net Advances, Gross NPAs and Net NPAs, as per the format in Annex - I

conditions:
If the debits arising out of devolvement of letters of credit or invoked guarantees
are parked in a separate account
requirements.full_sentence:
If the debits arising out of devolvement of letters of credit or invoked guarantees
are parked in a separate account, the balance outstanding in that account also
should be treated as a part of the borrower's principal operating account for the
purpose of application of these Directions.
requirements.object: part of the borrower's principal operating account for the purpose of application of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall classify a loan or an advance as NPA if any of the following
conditions is satisfied:
(1) interest and / or instalment of principal remains overdue for a period of
more than 90 days in respect of a term loan;
requirements.object: an advance as NPA if any of the following conditions is satisfied: (1) interest and / or instalment of principal remains overdue fo

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that in cases where the contract provides for settlement of the
current mark-to-market (MTM) value of a derivative contract before its
maturity, only the current credit exposure (not the potential future
exposure) shall be classified as a non-performing asset after an overdue
period of 90 days;
requirements.object: non-performing asset after an overdue period of 90 days

conditions:
where a bank partially or fully terminates
the derivative contract before maturity

if the amount becomes overdue for 90 days from the date of partial / full
termination of the derivative contract
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided further that in cases where a bank partially or fully terminates
the derivative contract before maturity, at their discretion, based on
preference of the clients to reduce the notional exposure of the hedging
derivative contract, and the bank permitted payment in instalments of the
crystallized MTM of such derivative contracts (including Forex Forward
Contracts), the recievable shall be classified as non-performing asset: (i)
if the amount becomes overdue for 90 days from the date of partial / full
termination of the derivative contract;
requirements.object: asset: (i) if the amount becomes overdue for 90 days from the date of partial / full termination of the derivative con

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the conditions in paragraph 29, an account may also be classified
as NPA in terms of certain specific provisions of this Chapter, including, inter alia,
paragraphs 37 to 47 as well as instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of
India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions,
2025.
requirements.object: NPA in terms of certain specific provisions of this Chapter, including, inter alia, paragraphs 37 to 47 as well as instructions

requirements.full_sentence: Asset classification shall be borrower-wise and not facility-wise.
requirements.object: facility-wise

requirements.full_sentence:
All the facilities
granted by an AIFI to a borrower and investment in all the securities issued by
the borrower shall have to be treated as NPA / Non-Performing Investment (NPI)
<image>
and not the particular facility / investment or part thereof which has become
irregular.
requirements.object: as NPA / Non-Performing Investment (NPI) <image> and not the particular facility / investment or part thereof which has become irre

requirements.full_sentence: The classification of an asset as NPA should be based on the record of recovery.
requirements.object: record of recovery

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI should not classify an advance account as NPA merely due to the
existence of some deficiencies which are temporary in nature.
requirements.object: as NPA merely due to the existence of some deficiencies which are temporary in nature

requirements.full_sentence:
The availability of security or net worth of borrower / guarantor should not be
taken into account for the purpose of treating an advance as NPA (except to the
extent provided in paragraph 52 or otherwise.
requirements.object: for the purpose of treating an advance as NPA (except to the extent provided in paragraph 52 or otherwise

requirements.full_sentence:
The asset classification of borrowal accounts where a solitary or a few credits
are recorded before the balance sheet date should be handled with care and
without scope for subjectivity.
requirements.object: and without scope for subjectivity

conditions:
Where the account indicates inherent weakness on the basis of the data
available
requirements.full_sentence:
Where the account indicates inherent weakness on the basis of the data
available, the account should be deemed as NPA.

In other genuine cases, an AIFI shall furnish satisfactory evidence to the
Statutory Auditors / Inspecting Officers about the manner of regularisation of the
account to eliminate doubts on their performing status.
requirements.object: the Statutory Auditors / Inspecting Officers about the manner of regularisation of the account to eliminate doubts on

requirements.full_sentence:
The bills discounted under Letter of Credit (LC) favouring a borrower may not be
classified as NPA, when any other facility granted to the borrower is classified as
NPA.

Notwithstanding paragraph 37, in case documents under LC are not accepted
on presentation or the payment under the LC is not made on the due date by the
LC issuing bank / AIFI for any reason and the borrower does not immediately
make good the amount disbursed as a result of discounting of concerned bills,
the outstanding bills discounted shall immediately be classified as NPA with
effect from the date when the other facilities had been classified as NPA.
requirements.object: NPA with effect from the date when the other facilities had been classified as NPA

conditions:
In case the overdues arising from forward contracts and plain vanilla
swaps and options become NPAs
requirements.full_sentence:
Derivative Contracts
<image>
(1) In case the overdues arising from forward contracts and plain vanilla
swaps and options become NPAs, all other funded facilities granted to
the client shall also be classified as NPA following the principle of
borrower-wise classification.
requirements.object: NPA following the principle of borrower-wise classification

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The income recognition in respect of the derivative contracts classified
as NPA shall be as per paragraph 114.

Advances under consortium arrangements
(1) Asset classification of accounts under consortium shall be based on the
record of recovery of the individual member and other aspects having a
bearing on the recoverability of the advances.
requirements.object: record of recovery of the individual member and other aspects having a bearing on the recoverability of the advances

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An AIFI participating in the consortium should, therefore, arrange to get
its share of recovery transferred from the lead bank or get an express
consent from the lead bank for the transfer of its share of recovery, to
ensure proper asset classification in its books.
requirements.object: get its share of recovery transferred from the lead bank or get an express consent from the lead bank for the transfer of its

requirements.full_sentence:
Such loans /
advances should be classified as NPA only when there is a default in
repayment of instalment of principal or payment of interest on the
respective due dates.
requirements.object: only when there is a default in repayment of instalment of principal or payment of interest on the respective due dates

requirements.full_sentence:
Agricultural advances
(1) Depending upon the duration of crops raised by an agriculturist, the NPA
norms as per sub-paragrpahs 29(3) and 29(4) shall also be made
applicable to agricultural term loans availed of by them.
requirements.object: to agricultural term loans availed of by them

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The NPA norms as per sub-paragrpahs 29(3) and 29(4) shall be made
applicable only to the following credit facilities extended for agricultural
activities:
requirements.object: to the following credit facilities extended for agricultural activities:

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) crop loans to farmers, which shall include traditional / non-
traditional plantations, and horticulture;
requirements.object: traditional plantations, and horticulture

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) crop loans to farmers which shall include traditional / non-
traditional plantations and horticulture;
requirements.object: traditional plantations and horticulture

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) In respect of agricultural loans, other than those specified in sub-
paragraph (2) above, identification of NPAs shall be done on the same
basis as non-agricultural advances, which at present is the 90 days
delinquency norm.
requirements.object: same basis as non-agricultural advances, which at present is the 90 days delinquency norm

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) While fixing the repayment schedule in case of rural housing advances
granted to agriculturists under Indira Awas Yojana / Pradhan Mantri
Gram Awas Yojana and Golden Jubilee Rural Housing Finance Scheme,
an AIFI shall ensure that the interest / instalment payable on such
advances are linked to crop cycles.
requirements.object: / instalment payable on such advances are linked to crop cycles

requirements.full_sentence:
Government guaranteed advances
(1) The credit facilities backed by guarantee of the Central Government
though overdue shall be treated as NPA only when the Government
repudiates its guarantee when invoked.
requirements.object: only when the Government repudiates its guarantee when invoked

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) State Government guaranteed advances and investments in State
Government guaranteed securities shall attract asset classification and
provisioning norms if interest and / or principal or any other amount due
to the AIFI remains overdue for more than 90 days.
requirements.object: provisioning norms if interest and / or principal or any other amount due to the AIFI remains overdue for more than 9

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In such cases, where the lending AIFI is able to establish through
documentary evidence that the importer has cleared the dues in full by
depositing the amount in the bank abroad before it turned into NPA in
<image>
the books of the AIFI, but the importer's country is not allowing the funds
to be remitted due to political or other reasons, the asset classification
may be made after a period of one year from the date the amount was
deposited by the importer in the bank abroad.
requirements.object: period of one year from the date the amount was deposited by the importer in the bank abroad

requirements.full_sentence:
Transfer of Loan Exposures - The asset classification and provisioning
requirements in respect of transactions involving transfer of loans shall be as per
the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Transfer and
Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Transfer and Distribution of Credit Risk) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
Takeout finance
(1) The asset classification norms shall be followed by the concerned AIFI
in whose books the account stands as balance sheet item as on the
relevant date.
requirements.object: concerned AIFI in whose books the account stands as balance sheet item as on the relevant date

conditions:
Where the credit facility becomes NPA before take over by the taking
over institution
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where the credit facility becomes NPA before take over by the taking
over institution, the lending AIFI shall classify the asset as NPA.
requirements.object: NPA

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Subsequent to take over by an AIFI, the taking over AIFI shall classify
the asset as NPA from the actual date of it becoming NPA even though
the account was not in its books as on that date.
requirements.object: NPA from the actual date of it becoming NPA even though the account was not in its books as on that date

requirements.full_sentence:
If a resolution plan is implemented in adherence to the provisions of Chapter
VI-A of the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution
of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025 dated November 28, 2025, borrower
accounts which are classified as 'Standard' may be retained as such upon
implementation.
requirements.object: upon implementation

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrower accounts which may have slipped into NPA between
the date of occurrence of the calamity and implementation of the resolution
plan, shall be upgraded as 'Standard', upon implementation of the resolution
plan.
requirements.object: upon implementation of the resolution plan

conditions: Provided that after implementation of the resolution plan
requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that after implementation of the resolution plan, the subsequent asset
classification shall be governed by the criteria laid out in these Directions.
requirements.object: criteria laid out in these Directions

conditions:
where a subsequent restructuring is
necessitated under the provisions of Chapter VI-A of the afore-mentioned
Directions
requirements.full_sentence:
Accounts which are restructured under paragraph 119I to 119Q of the Reserve
Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets)
Directions, 2025 dated November 28, 2025, where a subsequent restructuring is
necessitated under the provisions of Chapter VI-A of the afore-mentioned
Directions, shall continue to be classified as 'Standard'.]
E.
requirements.object: as 'Standard'

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall classify non-performing assets further into 'substandard assets',
'doubtful assets' and 'loss assets' categories based on the period for which the
asset has remained non-performing and the realisability of the dues.
requirements.object: into 'substandard assets', 'doubtful assets' and 'loss assets' categories based on the period for which the ass

requirements.full_sentence:
An NPA classified as loss asset is considered uncollectible and of such little value
that its continuance as a bankable asset is not warranted although there may be
some salvage or recovery value.
requirements.object: recovery value

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 33, in respect of accounts where there are potential
threats for recovery on account of erosion in the value of security or non-
availability of security and existence of other factors such as frauds committed
by borrowers, the asset should be straightaway classified as doubtful or loss
asset as appropriate.
requirements.object: doubtful or loss asset as appropriate

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of paragraph 52, the following shall apply:
(1) erosion in the value of security shall be reckoned as significant when the
realisable value of the security is less than fifty per cent of the value
assessed by the AIFI or accepted by the Reserve Bank at the time of
last inspection, as the case may be.
requirements.object: the value of security shall be reckoned as significant when the realisable value of the security is less than fifty per cent of

requirements.full_sentence:
Such NPAs may be straightaway
classified under doubtful category.
requirements.object: doubtful category

conditions: if the realisable value of the security
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) if the realisable value of the security, as assessed by the AIFI / approved
valuers / the Reserve Bank is less than ten per cent of the outstanding
in the borrowal accounts, the existence of security shall be ignored and
the asset shall be straightaway classified as loss asset.
requirements.object: asset shall be straightaway classified as loss asset

requirements.full_sentence:
The loan accounts classified as NPAs may be upgraded as 'standard' asset only
if entire arrears of interest and principal are paid by the borrower.
requirements.object: asset only if entire arrears of interest and principal are paid by the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
With regard to upgradation of accounts classified as NPA due to restructuring,
non-achievement of date of commencement of commercial operations (DCCO),
etc., the instructions as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025 shall be applicable
in addition to compliance with these Directions.
requirements.object: to compliance with these Directions

conditions: In case of borrowers having more than one credit facility from an AIFI
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of borrowers having more than one credit facility from an AIFI, loan
accounts shall be upgraded from NPA to standard asset category only upon
repayment of entire arrears of interest and principal pertaining to all the credit
facilities.
requirements.object: to standard asset category only upon repayment of entire arrears of interest and principal pertaining to all the credit facilitie

requirements.full_sentence: An AIFI resorting to partial and technical write-offs shall not show the remaining

The provisioning shall be made on the basis of the classification of assets based
on the period for which the asset has remained non-performing and the
availability of security and the realisable value thereof.
requirements.object: basis of the classification of assets based on the period for which the asset has remained non-performing and the availability of secur

requirements.full_sentence:
Taking into account the time lag between an account becoming doubtful of
recovery, its recognition as such, the realisation of the security and the erosion
over time in the value of security charged to the AIFI, the AIFI shall make
provision against substandard assets, doubtful assets and loss assets.
requirements.object: assets, doubtful assets and loss assets

requirements.full_sentence:
The rights, licenses, authorisations, etc., charged to the AIFI as collateral in
respect of projects (including infrastructure projects) financed by them, should
not be reckoned as tangible security.
requirements.object: security

requirements.full_sentence:
Hence such advances shall be reckoned
as unsecured.

conditions:
where there are provisions to
compensate the project sponsor if a certain level of traffic is not achieved
requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI may treat annuities under build-operate-transfer (BOT) model in respect
of road / highway projects and toll collection rights, where there are provisions to
compensate the project sponsor if a certain level of traffic is not achieved, as
tangible securities subject to the condition that the right of the AIFI to receive
annuities and toll collection rights is legally enforceable and irrevocable.
requirements.object: (BOT) model in respect of road / highway projects and toll collection rights

conditions: In case of public-private partnership (PPP) projects
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of public-private partnership (PPP) projects, the debts due to an AIFI may
be considered as secured to the extent assured by the project authority in terms
of the Concession Agreement, subject to the following conditions:
<image>
(1) user charges / toll / tariff payments are kept in an escrow account where
senior lenders have priority over withdrawals by the concessionaire;
requirements.object: to the extent assured by the project authority in terms of the Concession Agreement

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) In all such cases, the AIFI shall satisfy itself about the legal enforceability
of the provisions of the tripartite agreement and factor in their past
experience with such contracts.
requirements.object: legal enforceability of the provisions of the tripartite agreement and factor in their past experience with such contracts
standard assets at the following rates for the funded outstanding on global loan portfolio basis: (1) farm credit to agricu

requirements.full_sentence:
Loans to Medium Enterprises shall attract 0.40 per cent standard asset
provisioning.
The provisions on standard assets shall not be reckoned for arriving at net NPAs.
requirements.object: at net NPAs

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall make an additional specific provision of five percent of the
outstanding debt in respect of borrowers, for whom a resolution plan has been
implemented in terms of Chapter VI-A of the Reserve Bank of India (All India
Financial Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: provision of five percent of the outstanding debt in respect of borrowers, for whom a resolution plan has been implemented

requirements.full_sentence:
The
additional specific provisions shall be over and above the applicable prudential
provisions subject to a ceiling of hundred per cent.
requirements.object: the applicable prudential provisions subject to a ceiling of hundred per cent

requirements.full_sentence:
For accounts where repeated restructuring is necessitated in terms of Chapter
VI-A of the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of
Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025, an AIFI shall make additional specific
provisioning of five per cent of the outstanding debt for each instance of
restructuring made under the aforesaid Directions.
requirements.object: of five per cent of the outstanding debt for each instance of restructuring made under the aforesaid Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
This additional specific
provisioning shall be over and above the applicable prudential provisions subject
to a ceiling of hundred per cent.
requirements.object: the applicable prudential provisions subject to a ceiling of hundred per cent

requirements.full_sentence:
The additional specific provisions maintained in terms of paragraph 68A and 68B
above may be written back upon the borrower paying at least 20% of the
outstanding debt with the bank, without slipping into NPA post implementation
of the restructuring, and without being subjected to another restructuring.
requirements.object: the borrower paying at least 20% of the outstanding debt with the bank, without slipping into NPA post implementation of the rest

conditions:
In case the
borrower defaults during the above period
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the
borrower defaults during the above period, the conditions for reversal of
additional specific provision, mentioned above, shall be tested from the date of
rectification of default.]
C.
requirements.object: date of rectification of default

requirements.full_sentence:
A general provision of 15 per cent on total outstanding should be made in respect
of substandard assets without making any allowance for Export Credit Guarantee
Corporation (ECGC) guarantee cover and securities available.
requirements.object: of substandard assets without making any allowance for Export Credit Guarantee Corporation (ECGC) guarantee cover and securities av

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 70, infrastructure loan accounts which are classified
as sub-standard shall attract a provisioning of 20 per cent, in view of certain
safeguards such as escrow accounts available in respect of infrastructure
lending.
requirements.object: 20 per cent, in view of certain safeguards such as escrow accounts available in respect of infrastructure lending

requirements.full_sentence:
To avail of this benefit of lower provisioning, an AIFI shall have in place
an appropriate mechanism to escrow the cash flows and also have a clear and
legal first claim on these cash flows.
requirements.object: appropriate mechanism to escrow the cash flows and also have a clear and legal first claim on these cash flows

requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of leased assets, the provisions shall be 15 per cent of the sum of
the net investment in the lease and the unrealised portion of finance income net
of finance charge component.
requirements.object: of the sum of the net investment in the lease and the unrealised portion of finance income net of finance charge component

requirements.full_sentence:
The terms 'net investment in the lease', 'finance
income' and 'finance charge' shall be as defined in 'AS 19 Leases'.
requirements.object: 'AS 19 Leases'

requirements.full_sentence:
Unsecured lease exposures, which are identified as 'substandard' shall attract
additional provision of 10 per cent, i.e., a total of 25 per cent.
requirements.object: 10 per cent, i

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall make provisions of 100 per cent of the extent to which the advance
is not covered by the realisable value of the security to which the AIFI has a valid
recourse and the realisable value is estimated on a realistic basis.
requirements.object: per cent of the extent to which the advance is not covered by the realisable value of the security to which the AIFI has a vali

requirements.full_sentence:
In regard to the secured portion, provision shall be made on the following basis,
at the rates ranging from 25 per cent to 100 per cent of the secured portion
depending upon the period for which the asset has remained doubtful:
Period for which the advance has remained in
Provisioning
requirement (in per cent) Up to one year 25 One to three years 40 More than three years 100
'doubtful' category
requirements.object: following basis, at the rates ranging from 25 per cent to 100 per cent of the secured portion depending upon the period for which the a

requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of leased assets classified as 'Doubtful', 100 per cent of the extent to
which the finance is not secured by the realisable value of the leased asset, shall
be provided for.
Realisable value must be estimated on a realistic basis.
requirements.object: realistic basis

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the above provision, provision at the rates specified in paragraph
75 shall be made on the sum of the net investment in the lease and the unrealised
portion of finance income net of finance charge component of the secured
portion, depending upon the period for which asset has been doubtful.
requirements.object: sum of the net investment in the lease and the unrealised portion of finance income net of finance charge component of the secured port

requirements.full_sentence: Loss assets should be written off.

conditions: If loss assets are permitted to remain in the books for any reason
requirements.full_sentence:
If loss assets are permitted to remain in the books for any reason, 100 per cent
of the outstanding should be provided for.

conditions: if for any reason
requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, if for any reason, a leased asset classified as 'Loss' is allowed to remain
in books, 100 per cent of the sum of the net investment in the lease and the
unrealised portion of finance income net of finance charge component shall be
provided for.

The Board approved policy for making provisions for standard assets at rates
higher than the regulatory minimum, shall be reviewed at least on a quarterly
basis, of the performance of various sectors of the economy to which the AIFI
has an exposure to evaluate the present and emerging risks and stress therein.
requirements.object: on a quarterly basis, of the performance of various sectors of the economy to which the AIFI has an exposure to evaluate the pres

requirements.full_sentence:
The review may include quantitative and qualitative aspects like debt-equity ratio,
interest coverage ratio, profit margins, ratings upgrade to downgrade ratio,
sectoral non-performing assets / stressed assets, industry performance and
outlook, legal / regulatory issues faced by the sector, etc.
requirements.object: aspects like debt-equity ratio, interest coverage ratio, profit margins, ratings upgrade to downgrade ratio, sect

requirements.full_sentence:
The reviews may also
include sector specific parameters.
requirements.object: parameters

requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, an AIFI may voluntarily make specific provisions for NPAs at rates
which are higher than the rates prescribed under these Directions, to provide for
estimated actual loss in collectible amount, provided such higher rates are
approved by the Board of Directors and consistently adopted from year to year.
requirements.object: for NPAs at rates which are higher than the rates prescribed under these Directions, to provide for estimated actual loss in collectible amount

requirements.full_sentence:
The additional provisions for NPAs, like the minimum regulatory provision on
NPAs, may be netted off from gross NPAs to arrive at the net NPAs.
requirements.object: gross NPAs to arrive at the net NPAs

conditions: in case of deposit accounts)
requirements.full_sentence:
Provisioning in respect of cases of fraud
(1) An AIFI shall normally provide for the entire amount due to the AIFI or
for which the AIFI is liable (including in case of deposit accounts),
immediately upon a fraud being detected.
requirements.object: entire amount due to the AIFI or for which the AIFI is liable (including in case of deposit accounts), immediately upon a fra

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) While computing the provisioning requirement, an AIFI may adjust
financial collateral eligible under credit risk mitigation techniques
available in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy) Directions, 2025,
<image>
if any, available with them with regard to the accounts declared as fraud
account.
requirements.object: under credit risk mitigation techniques available in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Inst

requirements.full_sentence:
Advances against deposits / specific instruments - advances against NSCs
eligible for surrender, KVPs, government & other securities and life insurance
policies shall attract provisioning requirements as applicable to their asset
classification status.
requirements.object: applicable to their asset classification status

requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of interest suspense account
(1) Amounts held in Interest Suspense Account shall not be reckoned as

(2) Amounts lying in the Interest Suspense Account shall be deducted from
the relative advances and thereafter, provisioning as per the norms,
should be made on the balances after such deduction.
requirements.object: relative advances and thereafter, provisioning as per the norms, should be made on the balances after such deduction

requirements.full_sentence:
Project Finance
(1) For project finance exposures, an AIFI shall maintain a general provision
at the following rates for the funded outstanding on a portfolio basis:
Construction Phase
Operational Phase
- after commencement
(in per cent)
of repayment of interest and principal
(in per cent) CRE 1.25 1.00 CRE-RH 1.00 0.75 All others 1.00 0.40
(2) For accounts which have availed DCCO deferment and are classified as
'standard', an AIFI shall maintain additional specific provisions as per the
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of
Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: at the following rates for the funded outstanding on a portfolio basis: Construction Phase Operational Phase - after comm

conditions:
where financial closure has been achieved as
on October 1
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The provisions stipulated in sub-paragraphs (1) and (2) above shall not
be applicable for projects where financial closure has been achieved as
on October 1, 2025, and such project loans shall continue to be guided
by the prudential guidelines prevailing before October 1, 2025 for the
purpose of provisioning, which otherwise shall be treated as repealed.
requirements.object: where financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025, and such project loans shall continue to be guided by the

conditions: if these were sanctioned post October 1
requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) Notwithstanding sub-paragraph (3) above, in case of any resolution of a
fresh credit event and / or change in material terms and conditions in the
loan contract in such projects, subsequent to October 1, 2025, the
provisions stipulated in sub-paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply to these
projects as if these were sanctioned post October 1, 2025.
requirements.object: as if these were sanctioned post October 1, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Provisioning for project loans classified as NPA shall be as per the
instructions in paragraphs 69 to 80 of these Directions.
requirements.object: instructions in paragraphs 69 to 80 of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
Advances covered by ECGC guarantee
(1) In the case of advances classified as doubtful and guaranteed by ECGC,
provision shall be made only for the balance in excess of the amount
guaranteed by the ECGC.
requirements.object: the balance in excess of the amount guaranteed by the ECGC

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Further, while arriving at the provision required to be made for doubtful
assets, realizable value of the securities shall first be deducted from the
outstanding balance in respect of the amount guaranteed by the ECGC
and then provision made.
requirements.object: the outstanding balance in respect of the amount guaranteed by the ECGC and then provision made

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The amount outstanding, in excess of the guaranteed portion, shall be
provided for as per the extant guidelines on provisioning for non-
performing assets.
requirements.object: per the extant guidelines on provisioning for non- performing assets

requirements.full_sentence:
Such assets should not normally be
revalued.

conditions:
In case such assets need to be revalued as per requirement
of accounting practices or for any other requirement
requirements.full_sentence:
In case such assets need to be revalued as per requirement
of accounting practices or for any other requirement, the following
procedure shall be adopted:

(i) The loss on revaluation of assets shall be booked in the Profit &
Loss Account of the AIFI.
requirements.object: Profit & Loss Account of the AIFI

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) In addition to the provisioning requirement as per asset
classification, the full amount of the Revaluation Gain, if any, on
account of foreign exchange fluctuation shall be used to make
provisions against the corresponding assets.
requirements.object: provisions against the corresponding assets

requirements.full_sentence:
Provisioning for country risk
(1) An AIFI shall make provisions, on the net funded country exposures on
a graded scale ranging from 0.25 to 100 per cent according to the risk
categories as per the following schedule:
Risk category ECGC Classification Provisioning Requirement (per
cent) Insignificant A1 0.25 Low Risk A2 0.25 Moderately Low Risk B1 5 Moderate Risk B2 20 Moderately High Risk C1 25 High Risk C2 100 Very High Risk D 100
(2) An AIFI shall make provision for country risk in respect of a country
where its net funded exposure is one per cent or more of its total assets.
requirements.object: net funded country exposures on a graded scale ranging from 0

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The provision for country risk shall be in addition to the provisions
required to be held according to the asset classification status of the
asset.
requirements.object: the provisions required to be held according to the asset classification status of the asset

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Notwithstanding sub-paragraph (3) above, in the case of 'loss assets'
and 'doubtful assets', provision held, including provision held for country
risk, shall not exceed 100 per cent of the outstanding.
requirements.object: of the outstanding

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(5) An AIFI may not make any provision for 'home country' exposures i.e.
requirements.object: 'home country' exposures i

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The exposures of foreign branches of AIFIs to the host country should
be included for the computation of provision requirements.
requirements.object: computation of provision requirements

requirements.full_sentence:
(7) An AIFI may make a lower level of provisioning (say 25 per cent of the
requirement) in respect of short-term exposures (i.e.
requirements.object: of provisioning (say 25 per cent of the requirement) in respect of short-term exposures (i

requirements.full_sentence:
Provisioning norms for liquidity facility provided for Securitisation transactions
(1) The amount of liquidity facility drawn and outstanding for more than 90
days, in respect of securitisation transactions undertaken in terms of the
Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Securitisation
Transactions) Directions, 2025, shall be fully provided for.

Provisioning requirements for derivative exposures
(1) Credit exposures computed as per the current marked to market value
of the contract, arising on account of the interest rate and foreign
exchange derivative transactions, credit default swaps and gold, shall
attract provisioning requirement as applicable to the loan assets in the
'standard' category, of the concerned counterparties.
requirements.object: applicable to the loan assets in the 'standard' category, of the concerned counterparties

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) All conditions applicable for treatment of the provisions for standard
assets shall also apply to the aforesaid provisions for derivative and gold
exposures.
requirements.object: aforesaid provisions for derivative and gold exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
at comparatively lower rates of interest in the
first few years, after which rates are reset at higher rates, shall be 2.00
per cent in view of the higher risk associated with them.

(2) The provisioning on these assets shall revert to 0.40 per cent after one
year from the date on which the rates are reset at higher rates if the
accounts remain 'standard'.

Wilful Defaulters
(1) The provisioning in respect of existing loans / exposures to companies
having director(s) (other than nominee directors of government /
financial institutions brought on board at the time of distress), whose
name(s) appear more than once in the list of wilful defaulters, shall be
five per cent in cases of standard accounts.
requirements.object: in cases of standard accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
Takeout finance
(1) The lending AIFI and / or the taking over AIFI, shall make appropriate
provisions corresponding to the asset classification as per paragraph 47.
requirements.object: to the asset classification as per paragraph 47

conditions: when the asset is taken over by the taking over institution
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) As and when the asset is taken over by the taking over institution, the
lending AIFI may reverse the corresponding provisions.

An AIFI shall recognise income on accrual basis only in respect of credit facilities
which are classified as 'standard'.
requirements.object: basis only in respect of credit facilities which are classified as 'standard'

requirements.full_sentence:
For credit faciliites which are not classified as 'standard' including those
guaranteed by Government, income shall be recognized on actual receipt basis
i.e.
requirements.object: receipt basis i

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 102, interest on advances against NSCs, KVPs, and
life insurance policies may be taken to income account on the due date, provided
adequate margin is available in the accounts.
requirements.object: account on the due date

requirements.full_sentence:
Fees and commissions earned by an AIFI as a result of renegotiations or
rescheduling of outstanding debts shall be recognised on an accrual basis over
the period of time covered by the renegotiated or rescheduled extension of credit.
requirements.object: accrual basis over the period of time covered by the renegotiated or rescheduled extension of credit

conditions: If any advance
requirements.full_sentence:
If any advance, including bills purchased and discounted as well as a
Government guaranteed account, becomes NPA, the entire interest accrued and
credited to income account in the past periods, shall be reversed if the same is
not realised.
requirements.object: same is not realised

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of NPAs, fees, commission and similar income that have accrued shall
cease to accrue in the current period and shall be reversed with respect to past
periods, if uncollected.
requirements.object: the current period and shall be reversed with respect to past periods

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of leased assets, the finance charge component of finance income (as
defined in 'AS 19 Leases') on the leased asset which has accrued and was
credited to income account before the asset became non-performing, and
<image>
remaining unrealised, shall be reversed or provided for in the current accounting
period.
requirements.object: for in the current accounting period

requirements.full_sentence:
On an account turning NPA, an AIFI shall reverse the interest already charged
and not collected by debiting Profit and Loss account and stop further application
of interest.
requirements.object: charged and not collected by debiting Profit and Loss account and stop further application of interest

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall continue to record such accrued interest in a Memorandum account
in their books.
requirements.object: accrued interest in a Memorandum account in their books

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of computing Gross Advances, interest recorded in the
Memorandum account should not be considered.

Interest realised on NPAs may be taken to income account provided the credits
in the accounts towards interest are not out of fresh / additional credit facilities
sanctioned to the borrower concerned.
requirements.object: account provided the credits in the accounts towards interest are not out of fresh / additional credit facilities sanctioned to the

requirements.full_sentence:
towards principal or interest
due), the AIFI shall adopt an accounting principle and exercise the right of
appropriation of recoveries in a uniform and consistent manner.
requirements.object: and exercise the right of appropriation of recoveries in a uniform and consistent manner

requirements.full_sentence:
Derivative contracts classified as NPA
(1) In respect of derivative contracts classified as NPA, the amount
representing unrealized income already booked on accrual basis and
taken to 'Profit and Loss a/c' shall be reversed and held in a 'Suspense
Account-Crystallised Receivables' in the same manner as done in the
case of overdue advances.
requirements.object: in a 'Suspense Account-Crystallised Receivables' in the same manner as done in the case of overdue advances

conditions:
If the derivative contract is not terminated on the overdue receivable
remaining unpaid for 90 days
requirements.full_sentence:
(3) If the derivative contract is not terminated on the overdue receivable
remaining unpaid for 90 days, in addition to reversing the crystallised
receivable from Profit and Loss Account as stipulated in sub-paragraph
(1) above, the positive MTM pertaining to future receivables shall also
be reversed from Profit and Loss Account to another account styled as
'Suspense Account - Positive MTM'.
requirements.object: Profit and Loss Account to another account styled as 'Suspense Account - Positive MTM'

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The subsequent positive changes in the MTM value shall be credited to
the 'Suspense Account - Positive MTM', not to Profit and Loss Account.
requirements.object: 'Suspense Account - Positive MTM', not to Profit and Loss Account

requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The subsequent decline in MTM value shall be adjusted against the
balance in 'Suspense Account - Positive MTM'.
requirements.object: balance in 'Suspense Account - Positive MTM'

conditions:
If the balance in this
account is not sufficient
requirements.full_sentence:
If the balance in this
account is not sufficient, the remaining amount may be debited to the
Profit and Loss Account.
requirements.object: Profit and Loss Account

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) On payment of the overdues in cash, the balance in the 'Suspense
Account-Crystallised Receivables' shall be transferred to the 'Profit and
Loss Account', to the extent payment is received.
requirements.object: 'Profit and Loss Account', to the extent payment is received

conditions: If the AIFI has other derivative exposures on the borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
(7) If the AIFI has other derivative exposures on the borrower, the MTMs of
other derivative exposures shall also be dealt with / accounted for in the
manner as described in this paragraph, subsequent to the crystallised /
settlement amount in respect of a particular derivative transaction being
treated as NPA.
requirements.object: / accounted for in the manner as described in this paragraph, subsequent to the crystallised / settlement amount in respect of a pa

conditions:
in case a fund-based credit facility extended to a borrower is
classified as NPA
requirements.full_sentence:
(8) Similarly, in case a fund-based credit facility extended to a borrower is
classified as NPA, the MTMs of all the derivative exposures should be
treated as per this Paragraph.
requirements.object: this Paragraph

requirements.full_sentence:
Take out finance
(1) The norms of income recognition shall be followed by the AIFI in whose
books the account stands as balance sheet item as on the relevant date.
requirements.object: AIFI in whose books the account stands as balance sheet item as on the relevant date

conditions:
Where the credit facility becomes NPA before take over by the taking
over institution
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Where the credit facility becomes NPA before take over by the taking
over institution, the lending AIFI should not recognise income on accrual
<image>
basis and account for the same only when it is paid by the borrower /
taking over institution (if the arrangement so provides).
requirements.object: <image> basis and account for the same only when it is paid by the borrower / taking over institution (if the arrangement

conditions: where moratorium has been granted for repayment of interest
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases of loans where moratorium has been granted for repayment of interest,
income may be recognised on accrual basis for accounts which continue to be
classified as 'standard'.
requirements.object: basis for accounts which continue to be classified as 'standard'

requirements.full_sentence:
This shall be evaluated against the definition of
'restructuring' provided in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial
Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: definition of 'restructuring' provided in the Reserve Bank of India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of Stresse

requirements.full_sentence:
Interest income recognition in respect of borrower accounts where resolution
plan has been implemented in terms of Chapter VI-A of the Reserve Bank of
India (All India Financial Institutions - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions,
2025 dated November 28, 2025, shall be on accrual basis.

For accounts specified at paragraph 68B of these Directions, interest income
shall be recognized on cash basis.]
F.
requirements.object: basis

requirements.full_sentence:
In terms of Section 43(D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, income by way of interest
in relation to such categories of bad and doubtful debts as may be prescribed
having regard to the guidelines issued by the Reserve Bank in relation to such
debts, shall be chargeable to tax in the previous year in which it is credited to the
profit and loss account or received, whichever is earlier.
requirements.object: in the previous year in which it is credited to the profit and loss account or received, whichever is earlier

requirements.full_sentence:
An AIFI shall either make full provision as per these Directions or write-off such
advances and claim such tax benefits as are applicable, by evolving appropriate
methodology in consultation with their auditors/tax consultants.
requirements.object: as per these Directions or write-off such advances and claim such tax benefits as are applicable, by evolving appropriate m

requirements.full_sentence:
Recoveries made in such accounts shall be offered for tax purposes as per the
rules.
requirements.object: purposes as per the rules

requirements.full_sentence:
The directions, instructions and guidelines already repealed vide any of the
directions, instructions, and guidelines listed in the above notification shall
continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may
be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or
punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or
guidelines had not been repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue
necessary clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the
interpretation of any provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank
shall be final and binding.
